directive_events.owner_directive: Authorized: fix the global Stop so cancelling a workflow terminates every external process an app block launched (PTY agents and external GUI apps such as Fiji), not just the worker.
directive_events.reason: Owner explicitly authorized admin-approved:core-change for bug 6 (global Stop must terminate every external process an app block launched: PTY agents and external GUI apps like Fiji). Fix touches protected engine/scheduler and app-block runtime.

directive_events.owner_directive: Bug 6: only make Stop responsive (no external-process kill). Bug 4 also: small->fullscreen resize must make the agent TUI redraw to fill the enlarged PTY.
directive_events.reason: Owner narrowed bug 6: do NOT kill external processes; only fix the post-Stop unresponsiveness (UI hangs on the worker terminate grace period). Fix is frontend-only optimistic feedback - no protected engine/blocks change. The previously authorized admin-approved:core-change is therefore no longer needed for this PR. Owner also extended bug 4: resizing a PTY from small to fullscreen grows the PTY but the TUI content stays small (resize/refit not propagating).

directive_events.owner_directive: A: spawn the agent PTY with the prompt as a CLI argument instead of typing it in. B: fix CompletionWatcher so a stale leftover output file no longer completes the block immediately.
directive_events.reason: Owner authorized core-change to fix AI block lifecycle: (A) deliver the prompt as a spawn argv argument (typed-replay + carriage-return does not submit in claude-code or codex), (B) baseline pre-existing expected-output files so the FileWatcher completion path does not fire on stale outputs from a previous run. Both in protected blocks/ai.

docs_events.rationale: Bug-fix batch (#1789) restoring intended behavior — execute validation error now 422 not 500, AI port editor subtype-aware filtering, AI PTY prompt submit + resize-on-join, Stop UI responsiveness. No public contract, schema, API surface, plugin contract, or documented spec/ADR behavior changed; these correct regressions against already-documented intent, so no docs/spec/changelog update is required.

owner_directive: Fix a batch of alpha-test bugs in ONE PR: (1) workflow execute 500 on validation failure should be 4xx; (3) AI block port editor only offers DataObject instead of all DataObject subtypes; (4) claude-code TUI garbled in PTY; (5) AI block prompt never submitted after spawn (claude-code/codex); (6) Stop on app/AI block hangs and does not kill the child. Image previewer (bug 2) is OUT OF SCOPE here: it belongs to the decoupled scistudio-blocks-imaging package; core must have no image support.

scope_events.reason: Owner authorized core-change to fix AI block lifecycle: (A) deliver the prompt as a spawn argv argument (typed-replay + carriage-return does not submit in claude-code or codex), (B) baseline pre-existing expected-output files so the FileWatcher completion path does not fire on stale outputs from a previous run. Both in protected blocks/ai.

Owner authorized core-change to fix AI block lifecycle: (A) deliver the prompt as a spawn argv argument (typed-replay + carriage-return does not submit in claude-code or codex), (B) baseline pre-existing expected-output files so the FileWatcher completion path does not fire on stale outputs from a previous run. Both in protected blocks/ai.

Root-cause fix for AI block prompt: claude's variadic --mcp-config swallowed the trailing prompt (exit 1 in ask mode; bypass worked only because the bypass flag separated them). Deliver the prompt as a spawn CLI arg via _spawn/spawn_claude/spawn_codex with a -- separator (uniform, no per-provider special-casing). Stop responsiveness: scheduler cancel now backgrounds worker terminate so cancel returns immediately. Adds spawn plumbing + scheduler scope and their tests.
